subject: [PATCH v4 08/10] fuse: support registered buffers as buffer pools in io-uring

2026-06-12.patch_summary: This patch adds support for registered buffers as buffer pools in io-uring, allowing servers to use an io-uring registered buffer as the buffer pool and eliminating the overhead of pinning/unpinning user pages per i/o request. The server must register the payload buffers through io-uring with the FUSE_URING_REGISTERED_BUFFERS init flag set, and follow standard protocol for using registered buffers. This patch also includes kernel support for registered buffers advertised to userspace via the FUSE_HAS_IO_URING_BUFPOOL init flag. Benchmarks show a 10% throughput improvement for direct randreads and other improvements for different types of reads.

reviews.author: Miklos Szeredi
reviews.summary: Reviewer Miklos Szeredi questioned the addition of a new parameter init_flags to the fuse_uring_bufpool_setup function without providing a specific reason or context.
reviews.raw_body:
Is there any particular reason for this?  Not saying this is bad, just
wondering...

Thanks,
Miklos

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary:
The author addressed Miklos Szeredi's concern about pinned pages being accounted for and limited by the kernel io-uring infrastructure, confirming that this is indeed handled.

Author addressed Miklos Szeredi's concern that registered buffers should be supported without the buffer pool feature, explaining that it would add complexity and reduce test surface area. Author believes servers wanting registered buffers also want buffer pools, so no wanted use case is being left out.
reviews.raw_body:
Yes, pinned pages are accounted and limited by the kernel io-uring
infrastructure (in io_account_mem() in io_uring/rsrc.c) when userspace
registers the buffers in io-uring.

---

There's no technical reason it can't but it keeps things simpler (with
the buffer pool, userspace can more optimally register the bufferpool
into one fixed slot since the buffers are contiguous), reduces the
surface area of configurations that need to be tested, and allows the
feature to be advertised under the FUSE_URING_BUFPOOL capability flag
during init instead of needing a separate flag. I think in practice,
servers that would want the benefits from registered buffers would
also want the benefits from buffer pools, so I don't think we're
leaving out a wanted use case.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-06-15.patch_summary: This patch adds support for registered buffers as buffer pools in io-uring for the fuse file system. It allows servers to use an io-uring registered buffer as the buffer pool, eliminating the overhead of pinning and unpinning user pages per I/O request. The server must register the payload buffers through io-uring and set specific flags during registration. This patch also includes kernel support for registered buffers advertised via a single umbrella flag. Benchmarks show a 10% throughput improvement for direct randreads, 4% for direct sequential reads, 8% for buffered randreads, and 6% for buffered sequential reads.
